title: RNA Library Preparation Protocol
author: Dakota Betz
materials: Carefully remove and discard the ethanol.

Amplify the library using the following thermocycling profile: A B C D 1 Step Temp. (°C) Duration Cycles 2 Initial denaturation 98 45 sec 1 3 Denaturation 98 15 sec **see table below 4 Annealing* 60 30 sec **see table below 5 Extension 72 30 sec **see table below 6 Final Extension 72 1 min 1 7 HOLD 4 (infinite) 1 *Optimization of the annealing temperature may be required for non- standard (i.e., other than Illumina TruSeq®) adapter/primer combinations. **See table below for recommended cycle numbers. Recommended library amplification cycles: A B 1 Quantity of starting material Number of cycles 2 50 – 100 ng 13 – 16 3 101 – 250 ng 11 – 14 4 251 – 500 ng 9 – 12 5 501 – 1000 ng 7 – 10
Proceed immediately to Library Amplification Cleanup .

steps.title: Before starting , equilibrate mRNA Capture...
steps.instruction: Before starting , equilibrate mRNA Capture Beads, mRNA Bead Binding Buffer, mRNA Bead Wash Buffer and Fragment, Prime and Elute Buffer (2X) to room temperature. Before use, beads must be washed with mRNA Bead Binding Buffer (purpose of this section). Note: This entire protocol takes approximately 8 hrs to complete. Ideally, master mixes for the various steps in the process should be prepared as required. For a streamlined protocol, a reagent master mix with a minimum of 10% excess is prepared for each of these enzymatic steps (use scale feature of this protocol to automatically scale accordingly with appropriate excess). Always ensure that KAPA Pure Beads and PEG/NaCl Solution are fully equilibrated to room temperature before use.

steps.title: Resuspend the mRNA Capture Beads thoroughly...
steps.instruction: Resuspend the mRNA Capture Beads thoroughly by pipetting up and down gently, or by using a vortex mixer on a low to medium speed setting to prevent foaming. High-speed vortexing or shaking should be avoided to prevent foaming . Refer to Guidelines & Warnings: mRNA Capture Beads for more information.

steps.title: Transfer 52
steps.instruction: Transfer 52.5 µL (50 μL + 5% excess for each library to be prepared) of the resuspended mRNA Capture Beads into an appropriate tube. When preparing multiple libraries, beads for up to 24 libraries (1260 μL) may be washed in a single tube. When preparing more than 24 libraries, wash beads in multiple batches. Please refer to Guidelines & Warnings: mRNA Capture Beads for additional recommendations regarding bulk bead washing.

steps.title: Place the tube on a magnet...
steps.instruction: Place the tube on a magnet and incubate at room temperature until the solution is clear. Carefully remove and discard the supernatant, and replace it with an equal volume of mRNA Bead Binding Buffer , 52.5 µL (scaled; 52.5 uL per library).

steps.title: Remove the tube from the magnet...
steps.instruction: Remove the tube from the magnet and resuspend the beads by pipetting up and down, or by low to medium speed vortexing. Be careful to avoid producing excessive foam.

steps.title: Place the tube on the magnet...
steps.instruction: Place the tube on the magnet and incubate at room temperature until the solution is clear. Carefully remove and discard the supernatant, and replace it with an equal volume of mRNA Bead Binding Buffer 52.5 µL (52.5 μL per library).

steps.title: Remove the tube from the magnet...
steps.instruction: Remove the tube from the magnet and resuspend the beads by pipetting up and down, or by low to medium speed vortexing. Be careful to avoid producing excessive foam.

steps.title: For each RNA sample to be...
steps.instruction: For each RNA sample to be captured, transfer 50 μL of resuspended mRNA Capture Beads into individual tubes or wells of a plate.

steps.title: Place the plate/tube(s) containing the mixture...
steps.instruction: Place the plate/tube(s) containing the mixture of mRNA Capture Beads and RNA, on a magnet and incubate at room temperature until the solution is clear. Remove and discard the supernatant.

steps.title: To each well/tube from step 8,...
steps.instruction: To each well/tube from step 8, add 50 μL of the appropriate RNA sample (in RNase-free water). Mix thoroughly by gently pipetting up and down several times.

steps.title: Place the plate/tube(s) in a thermocycler...
steps.instruction: Place the plate/tube(s) in a thermocycler and perform the 1st mRNA capture as follows: A B C 1 Step Temperature Duration 2 1st mRNA capture 65°C 2 min 3 Cool 20°C 5 min

steps.title: Place the plate/tube(s) containing the mixture...
steps.instruction: Place the plate/tube(s) containing the mixture of mRNA Capture Beads and RNA, on a magnet and incubate at room temperature until the solution is clear. Remove and discard the supernatant.

steps.title: Remove the plate/tube(s) from the magnet...
steps.instruction: Remove the plate/tube(s) from the magnet and resuspend thoroughly in 200 μL of mRNA Bead Wash Buffer by pipetting up and down several times.

steps.title: Place the plate/tube(s) on the magnet...
steps.instruction: Place the plate/tube(s) on the magnet and incubate at room temperature until the solution is clear. Remove and discard the supernatant.

steps.title: Resuspend the beads in 50 μL of RNase-free water
steps.instruction: Resuspend the beads in 50 μL of RNase-free water .

steps.title: Place the plate/tube(s) in a thermocycler...
steps.instruction: Place the plate/tube(s) in a thermocycler and perform the 2nd mRNA capture as follows: A B C 1 Step Temperature Duration 2 2nd mRNA capture 70°C 2 min 3 Cool 20°C 5 min

steps.title: Add 50 μL of Bead Binding...
steps.instruction: Add 50 μL of Bead Binding Buffer to the mixture of mRNA Capture Beads and RNA in each tube, and mix thoroughly by gently pipetting up and down several times.

steps.title: Incubate the plate/tube(s) at 20°C for...
steps.instruction: Incubate the plate/tube(s) at 20°C for 5 min (timer available here: 00:05:00 ).

steps.title: Place the plate/tube(s) on the magnet...
steps.instruction: Place the plate/tube(s) on the magnet and incubate at room temperature until the solution is clear. Remove and discard the supernatant.

steps.title: Remove the beads from the magnet...
steps.instruction: Remove the beads from the magnet and resuspend in 200 μL of mRNA Bead Wash Buffer by pipetting up and down several times.

steps.title: Place the plate/tube(s) on the magnet...
steps.instruction: Place the plate/tube(s) on the magnet and incubate at room temperature until the solution is clear. Remove and discard the entire volume of supernatant. Caution : carryover of mRNA Bead Wash Buffer may inhibit 1st strand cDNA synthesis.

steps.title: Prepare the required volume of Fragment,...
steps.instruction: Prepare the required volume of Fragment, Prime and Elute Buffer (1X) at room temperature as follows: A B 1 Component Volume per sample (uL) 2 RNase-free water 11 3 Fragment, Prime and Elute Buffer (2X) 11 4 Total volume: 22 Volumes in this table are per sample. If you've scaled this protocol, your master mix (based on the above table) will need 11 µL, RNase-free water and 11 µL, Fragment, Prime & Elute Buffer (2X) .

steps.title: Thoroughly resuspend the mRNA Capture Beads...
steps.instruction: Thoroughly resuspend the mRNA Capture Beads with captured mRNA prepared in step 20 in 22 μL of Fragment, Prime and Elute Buffer (1X) prepared in step 21. Resuspended beads with captured mRNA may be stored at 4°C for ≤24 hrs . Do not freeze the samples as this will damage the beads. When ready, proceed to step 23. SAFE STOPPING POINT

steps.title: Place the plate/tube(s) in a thermocycler...
steps.instruction: Place the plate/tube(s) in a thermocycler and carry out the fragmentation and priming program given in the table below: A B 1 Desired mean library insert size (bp) Fragmentation 2 100 - 200 8 min at 94°C 3 200 - 300 6 min at 94°C 4 300 - 400 6 min at 85°C

steps.title: Immediately place the plate/tube(s) on a...
steps.instruction: Immediately place the plate/tube(s) on a magnet to capture the beads, and incubate until the liquid is clear. Caution : to prevent hybridization of poly(A)-rich RNA to the capture beads, do not allow the sample to cool before placing on the magnet.

steps.title: Carefully remove 20μL of the supernatant...
steps.instruction: Carefully remove 20μL of the supernatant containing the eluted, fragmented, and primed RNA into a separate plate or tube.

steps.title: Place the plate/tube(s) on ice and...
steps.instruction: Place the plate/tube(s) on ice and proceed immediately to 1st Strand Synthesis .

steps.title: On ice, assemble the 1st strand...
steps.instruction: On ice, assemble the 1st strand synthesis reaction as follows (see table). 1st strand synthesis master mix contains 11 µL, 1st Strand Synthesis Buffer + 1 µL, KAPA Script (scaled; per sample + 20% excess is 11 uL 1st SSB and 1 uL KAPA Script). A B 1 Component Volume 2 Fragmented, primed RNA eluted from beads 20 μL 3 1st strand synthesis master mix 10 μL 4 Total volume 30 μL Volumes in this table are per sample. Keeping the plate/tube(s) on ice, mix thoroughly by gently pipetting the reaction up and down several times.

steps.title: Incubate the plate/tube(s) using the following protocol
steps.instruction: Incubate the plate/tube(s) using the following protocol: A B C 1 Step Temperature (°C) Duration 2 Primer extension 25 10 min 3 1st strand synthesis 42 15 min 4 Enzyme inactivation 70 15 min 5 HOLD 4 (infinite) Place the plate/tube(s) on ice and proceed immediately to 2nd Strand Synthesis and A-tailing .

steps.title: On ice, assemble the 2nd strand...
steps.instruction: On ice, assemble the 2nd strand synthesis and A-tailing reaction as follows (see table). 2nd strand synthesis and A-tailing master mix contains 31 µL, 2nd Strand Marking Buffer + 2 µL, 2nd Strand Syn. & A-Tailing Enzyme Mix (scaled; per sample + 10% excess is 31 uL 2nd Strand Marking Buffer and 2 uL 2nd Strand Syn. & A-Tailing Enzyme Mix). A B 1 Component Volume 2 1st strand synthesis product 30 μL 3 2nd strand synthesis and A-tailing master mix 30 μL 4 Total volume 60 μL Volumes in this table are per sample. Keeping the plate/tube(s) on ice, mix thoroughly by gently pipetting the reaction up and down several times.

steps.title: Incubate the plate/tube(s) using the following protocol
steps.instruction: Incubate the plate/tube(s) using the following protocol: A B C 1 Step Temp. (°C) Duration 2 2nd strand synthesis 16 30 min 3 A-tailing 62 10 min 4 HOLD 4 (infinite) Place the plate/tube(s) on ice and proceed immediately to Adapter Ligation .

steps.title: Dilute adapters in preparation for ligation,...
steps.instruction: Dilute adapters in preparation for ligation, targeting the following concentrations: A B 1 Quantity of starting material Adapter stock concentration 2 50 – 499 ng 1.5 μM 3 500 – 1000 ng 7 μM

steps.title: On ice, set up the adapter...
steps.instruction: On ice, set up the adapter ligation reaction as follows (see table). Adapter ligation master mix contains 40 µL, Ligation Buffer + 10 µL, DNA Ligase (scaled; per sample + 10% excess is 40 uL Lig. Buffer and 10 uL Ligase). A B 1 Component Volume 2 2nd strand synthesis product 60 μL 3 Adapter ligation master mix 45 μL 4 Diluted adapter stock 5 μL 5 Total volume 110 μL Volumes in this table are per sample. Keeping the plate/tube(s) on ice, mix thoroughly by pipetting the reaction up and down several times.

steps.title: Incubate the plate/tube(s) at 20°C for...
steps.instruction: Incubate the plate/tube(s) at 20°C for 15 min (timer available here: 00:15:00 ). Proceed immediately to 1st Post-ligation Cleanup .

steps.title: Perform a 0
steps.instruction: Perform a 0.63X bead-based cleanup by combining the following: A B 1 Component Volume 2 Adapter-ligated DNA 110 μL 3 KAPA Pure Beads 70 μL 4 Total volume: 180 μL Volumes in this table are per sample. Mix thoroughly by vortexing and/or pipetting up and down multiple times.

steps.title: Incubate the plate/tube(s) at room temperature...
steps.instruction: Incubate the plate/tube(s) at room temperature for 5-15 mins to bind DNA to the beads. Optional timers available here: 00:05:00 00:10:00 00:15:00

steps.title: Place the plate/tube(s) on a magnet to capture the beads
steps.instruction: Place the plate/tube(s) on a magnet to capture the beads. Incubate until the liquid is clear.

steps.title: Carefully remove and discard 175 μL...
steps.instruction: Carefully remove and discard 175 μL of supernatant (from each tube).

steps.title: Keeping the plate/tube(s) on the magnet,...
steps.instruction: Keeping the plate/tube(s) on the magnet, add 200 μL of 80% ethanol (to each tube).

steps.title: Incubate the plate/tube(s) on the magnet...
steps.instruction: Incubate the plate/tube(s) on the magnet at room temperature for ≥ 30s (timer available here: 00:00:30 ).

steps.title: Keeping the plate/tube(s) on the magnet,...
steps.instruction: Keeping the plate/tube(s) on the magnet, add 200 μL of 80% ethanol (to each tube).

steps.title: Incubate the plate/tube(s) on the magnet...
steps.instruction: Incubate the plate/tube(s) on the magnet at room temperature for ≥ 30s (timer available here: 00:00:30 ).

steps.title: Carefully remove and discard the ethanol
steps.instruction: Carefully remove and discard the ethanol. Try to remove all residual ethanol without disturbing the beads with a toothpick.

steps.title: Dry the beads at room temperature...
steps.instruction: Dry the beads at room temperature for 3 – 5 min (timer: 00:05:00 ), or until all of the ethanol has evaporated. Caution: over-drying the beads may result in reduced yield.

steps.title: Remove the plate/tube(s) from the magnet
steps.instruction: Remove the plate/tube(s) from the magnet. Thoroughly resuspend the beads in 50 μL of 10 mM Tris-HCl (pH 8.0 – 8.5) per tube.

steps.title: Incubate the plate/tube(s) at room temperature...
steps.instruction: Incubate the plate/tube(s) at room temperature for 2 min to elute DNA off the beads (timer: 00:02:00 ). The solution with resuspended beads can be stored at 4°C for ≤24 hrs . Do not freeze the beads, as this can result in dramatic loss of DNA. When ready, proceed to 2nd Post-ligation Cleanup . SAFE STOPPING POINT

steps.title: Perform a 0.7X bead-based cleanup by combining the following
steps.instruction: Perform a 0.7X bead-based cleanup by combining the following: A B 1 Component Volume 2 Beads with purified, adapter-ligated DNA 50 μL 3 PEG/NaCl Solution 35 μL 4 Total volume: 85 μL Volumes in this table are per sample. Mix thoroughly by vortexing and/or pipetting up and down multiple times.

steps.title: Incubate the plate/tube(s) at room temperature...
steps.instruction: Incubate the plate/tube(s) at room temperature for 5-15 mins to bind DNA to the beads. Optional timers available here: 00:05:00 00:10:00 00:15:00

steps.title: Place the plate/tube(s) on a magnet to capture the beads
steps.instruction: Place the plate/tube(s) on a magnet to capture the beads. Incubate until the liquid is clear.

steps.title: Carefully remove and discard 80 μL...
steps.instruction: Carefully remove and discard 80 μL of supernatant (from each tube).

steps.title: Keeping the plate/tube(s) on the magnet,...
steps.instruction: Keeping the plate/tube(s) on the magnet, add 200 μL of 80% ethanol (to each tube).

steps.title: Incubate the plate/tube(s) on the magnet...
steps.instruction: Incubate the plate/tube(s) on the magnet at room temperature for ≥ 30s (timer: 00:00:30 ).

steps.title: Keeping the plate/tube(s) on the magnet,...
steps.instruction: Keeping the plate/tube(s) on the magnet, add 200 μL of 80% ethanol (to each tube).

steps.title: Incubate the plate/tube(s) on the magnet...
steps.instruction: Incubate the plate/tube(s) on the magnet at room temperature for ≥ 30s (timer: 00:00:30 ).

steps.title: Carefully remove and discard the ethanol
steps.instruction: Carefully remove and discard the ethanol. Try to remove all residual ethanol without disturbing the beads.

steps.title: Dry the beads at room temperature...
steps.instruction: Dry the beads at room temperature for 3 – 5 min (timer: 00:05:00 ), or until all of the ethanol has evaporated. Caution: over-drying the beads may result in reduced yield.

steps.title: Remove the plate/tube(s) from the magnet
steps.instruction: Remove the plate/tube(s) from the magnet. Thoroughly resuspend the beads in 22μL of 10 mM Tris-HCl (pH 8.0 – 8.5) per tube.

steps.title: Incubate the plate/tube(s) at room temperature...
steps.instruction: Incubate the plate/tube(s) at room temperature for 2 min to elute DNA off the beads (timer: 00:02:00 ).

steps.title: Place the plate/tube(s) on a magnet to capture the beads
steps.instruction: Place the plate/tube(s) on a magnet to capture the beads. Incubate until the liquid is clear.

steps.title: Transfer 20 μL of the clear...
steps.instruction: Transfer 20 μL of the clear supernatant to a new plate/tube(s). The purified, adapter-ligated library DNA may be stored at 4°C for ≤1 week , or frozen at -20°C for ≤1 month . When ready, proceed to Library Amplification . SAFE STOPPING POINT

steps.title: Assemble each library amplification reaction as follows (see table)
steps.instruction: Assemble each library amplification reaction as follows (see table). Library amplification master mix contains 27.5 µL, KAPA HiFi HotStart ReadyMix (2X) + 5.5 µL, Library Amplification Primer Mix (10X) including excess (scaled; per sample without excess is 25 μL KAPA HiFi and 5 μL Primer Mix). A B 1 Component Volume 2 Purified, adapter-ligated DNA 20 μL 3 Library amplification master mix 30 μL 4 Total volume: 50 μL Volumes in this table are per sample. Mix well by pipetting up and down several times.

steps.title: Perform a 1X bead-based cleanup by combining the following
steps.instruction: Perform a 1X bead-based cleanup by combining the following: A B 1 Component Volume 2 Adapter-ligated DNA 50 μL 3 KAPA Pure Beads 50 μL 4 Total volume: 100 μL Volumes in this table are per sample. Mix thoroughly by vortexing and/or pipetting up and down multiple times.

steps.title: Incubate the plate/tube(s) at room temperature...
steps.instruction: Incubate the plate/tube(s) at room temperature for 5-15 mins to bind DNA to the beads. Optional timers available here: 00:05:00 00:10:00 00:15:00

steps.title: Place the plate/tube(s) on a magnet to capture the beads
steps.instruction: Place the plate/tube(s) on a magnet to capture the beads. Incubate until the liquid is clear.

steps.title: Carefully remove and discard 95 μL...
steps.instruction: Carefully remove and discard 95 μL of supernatant (from each tube).

steps.title: Keeping the plate/tube(s) on the magnet,...
steps.instruction: Keeping the plate/tube(s) on the magnet, add 200 μL of 80% ethanol to each tube.

steps.title: Incubate the plate/tube(s) on the magnet...
steps.instruction: Incubate the plate/tube(s) on the magnet at room temperature for ≥ 30s (timer: 00:00:30 ).

steps.title: Carefully remove and discard the ethanol
steps.instruction: Carefully remove and discard the ethanol. Try to remove all residual ethanol without disturbing the beads.

steps.title: Dry the beads at room temperature...
steps.instruction: Dry the beads at room temperature for 3 – 5 min (timer: 00:05:00 ), or until all of the ethanol has evaporated. Caution: over-drying the beads may result in reduced yield.

steps.title: Thoroughly resuspend the dried beads in...
steps.instruction: Thoroughly resuspend the dried beads in 22 μL of 10 mM Tris-HCl (pH 8.0 – 8.5) per tube.

steps.title: Incubate the plate/tube(s) at room temperature...
steps.instruction: Incubate the plate/tube(s) at room temperature for 2 min to elute DNA off the beads (timer: 00:02:00 ).

steps.title: Place the plate/tube(s) on the magnet to capture the beads
steps.instruction: Place the plate/tube(s) on the magnet to capture the beads. Incubate until the liquid is clear.

steps.title: Transfer 20 μL of the clear...
steps.instruction: Transfer 20 μL of the clear supernatant to a new plate/tube(s), and store the purified, amplified libraries at 4°C for ≤ 1 week or at -20°C . END PROTOCOL (SAFE STOPPING POINT)
